metadata.description: Ego vehicle merges onto I-880 northbound from on-ramp during moderate traffic. Target gap is 4 seconds. Adjacent vehicle accelerates during merge window.

relevant_laws.description: A merging vehicle must yield the right-of-way to traffic already on the freeway.
No person shall drive at such a slow speed as to impede or block the normal and reasonable movement of traffic.
Whenever a highway lane ends and the driver is required to merge, the driver shall yield to traffic in the continuing lane.
Safe following distance must be maintained so that the driver can stop safely.

legality_research.research_summary: 4 applicable law(s) found in San Jose, California, United States for 'highway_merge' scenarios.

verdict.behavior_description: Ego vehicle merges onto I-880 northbound from on-ramp during moderate traffic but accelerates too quickly causing adjacent vehicle to adjust speed, violating minimum following distance law (CA VC 21703).
verdict.reasoning: Based on human factors and traffic laws analysis the driver of ego vehicle did not maintain a safe following distance which caused the adjacent vehicle to adjust its speed. This is in violation of CA VC 21703.
legal_violations: Violation: Excessive Speed, Law: California Vehicle Code Section 22400 (Minimum Speed)
metric_blind_spots: Metric: Collision detected, Actual behavior: Collision almost occurred due to excessive speed and merging too quickly
verdict.recommendations: The driver should slow down during the merge window and adjust speed according to the adjacent vehicle. The ego vehicle should also keep a safe following distance from the adjacent vehicle.
